verses.text: Elihu asks a little more patience, promising to speak on God's behalf and fetch his knowledge 'from afar.' He claims to plead God's cause. To defend God rightly is a high calling, to be undertaken with humility.

Elihu asks a little more patience, promising to speak on God's behalf and fetch his knowledge 'from afar.' He claims to plead God's cause. To defend God rightly is a high calling, to be undertaken with humility.

God is mighty yet 'despiseth not any,' and keeps His eyes on the righteous, setting them with kings. His power is joined to care. The great God does not overlook the lowliest of His faithful ones.
God is mighty yet 'despiseth not any,' and keeps His eyes on the righteous, setting them with kings. His power is joined to care. The great God does not overlook the lowliest of His faithful ones.
God is mighty yet 'despiseth not any,' and keeps His eyes on the righteous, setting them with kings. His power is joined to care. The great God does not overlook the lowliest of His faithful ones.
If the godly are bound in affliction, God uses it to show them their faults and open their ear to instruction. Suffering is His schoolroom. He afflicts His children to teach, not to destroy.
If the godly are bound in affliction, God uses it to show them their faults and open their ear to instruction. Suffering is His schoolroom. He afflicts His children to teach, not to destroy.
If the godly are bound in affliction, God uses it to show them their faults and open their ear to instruction. Suffering is His schoolroom. He afflicts His children to teach, not to destroy.
If they hear and obey, they end their days in good; if not, they perish without knowledge. Obedience is the path to blessing. The same trial that refines the humble hardens the rebellious.
If they hear and obey, they end their days in good; if not, they perish without knowledge. Obedience is the path to blessing. The same trial that refines the humble hardens the rebellious.

The hypocrites store up wrath and do not cry when bound, but God 'delivereth the poor in his affliction' and opens their ears by adversity. Trouble that embitters the proud instructs the humble. God speaks to the afflicted who will listen.

God would have drawn Job into a broad, rich place, Elihu warns; let him not despise the discipline or trust in ransom-money. He urges Job to submit. No wealth can buy escape from God's correcting hand.

Desire not the night of death, says Elihu; God is exalted in power, and 'who teacheth like him?' None can dictate His ways. The Master Teacher needs no instruction from the pupils He instructs.

Remember to magnify God's work, which men behold and praise; 'God is great, and we know him not,' His years beyond all reckoning. His greatness exceeds our grasp. The right response to mystery is worship, not complaint.

God draws up the drops of water and distils them as rain; who can understand the spreading of the clouds? Even the weather defies our wisdom. The skies daily display a power we can watch but never fathom.

He spreads His lightning, covers the deep, and by these feeds the nations and judges them; the very thunder declares His coming. The storm preaches His majesty. Nature itself summons us to reverence the God behind it.
